method_atom: coherent optical control of a two-level (or effective two-state) quantum system via resonant excitation with circularly-polarized pulses, creating a coherent superposition whose generation efficiency is maximized for odd-integer multiples of a pi-area pulse; the resulting coherence precesses about a transverse field and is read out through a polarization-rotation probe of the driven state.
object_atom: the electron spin of a single resident electron in n-type modulation-doped (In,Ga)As/GaAs semiconductor quantum dots, coupled optically to a trion state and precessing in an applied transverse magnetic field.
brief_reason: the pulse-area-controlled resonant superposition and precession/optical-readout machinery is transferable to any driven two-state quantum system, while the specific carrier is the single-electron spin in a semiconductor quantum dot.
